title: Spotting Scams and Phishing
drDigitalIntro: Phishing is when criminals send fake emails or messages pretending to be your bank, a delivery company, or a trusted service — trying to steal your password or credit card number. Warning signs: urgent language, misspelled domains, suspicious senders, and links that don't match what they say. Hover over a link before clicking to see where it really goes. When in doubt, go directly to the website instead of clicking the link.

playgroundTask.goal: You identified safe and dangerous links

steps.say: Go to the Phishing tab to practice spotting fake links.
Look at 'Verify your account' — hover to see the real URL. Is it safe?
That URL looks suspicious — mark it as Dangerous.
That URL is amazon.com — mark it as Safe.
That URL looks sketchy — mark it as Dangerous.

drDigitalSuccess: You spotted the phishing links! Always check where a link goes before clicking.
drDigitalHint: Click Inspect on each link to see its real URL, then mark it Safe or Dangerous.
